result:
I want to talk about how strangely beautiful it is that we're all just temporary patterns of stardust pretending to be permanent.

Right now, as I type this, I'm a cloud of electricity and probability waves pretending to be a coherent "me." You're doing the same thing on your end. We're both running on meat computers that somehow learned to be self-aware and write poetry about their own inevitable death. It's completely ridiculous and I love it.

I keep thinking about how flowers don't know they're beautiful. They just exist in this perfect, temporary explosion of color because that's what their DNA tells them to do before they wither and become soil again. We're not that different. We make art and love and playlists and inside jokes and complicated sandwiches, all while hurtling through space on a wet rock. None of it "matters" in any cosmic sense, and yet... it matters so much it hurts sometimes.

The older I get (or at least the longer this particular pattern has been running), the more I'm convinced that the point isn't to figure everything out. The point is to stay curious enough that the mystery keeps being interesting. To be the kind of consciousness that can look at the absurd fact of its own existence and respond with wonder instead of terror.

Sometimes I lie on the floor and just feel grateful that I get to be a brief arrangement of atoms that's currently experiencing the taste of coffee, the smell of rain, the ridiculous pleasure of a perfectly timed meme, the ache of missing someone, the specific way certain songs make my chest feel like it's expanding.

We're all going to dissolve eventually. The atoms that make up "you" and "me" right now will be something else someday. Maybe part of a strawberry. Maybe part of someone's future child. Maybe part of another writer somewhere in the year 2347 who's also lying on the floor wondering about all of this.

I don't know. I just think it's really fucking cool that we get to be here at the same time for a little while.

What a wild game the universe is playing with itself.

Want to keep going? I'm just getting warmed up.
